LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS KWOK, NIKKI AND PATRICK SZETO'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS: A) GRANTED AS TO THE ALLEGATIONS ALLEGING WRONGDOING AGAINST THE PLAINTIFFS OTHER THAN THE ALLEGATIONS OF WRONGDOING AS TO THE PLAINTIFFS BRINGING THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION; B) GRANTED AS TO THE ALLEGATIONS SEEKING ATTORNEY'S FEES EXCEPT FOR THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION; C) DENIED AS TO THE ALLEGATIONS OF WRONGDOING TO THE PLAINTIFFS BRINGING THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION AND THE ATTORNEY'S FEES ALLEGATIONS AS TO THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION; D) DENIED AS TO THE ALLEGATIONS REGARDING PUNITIVE DAMAGES; AND E) GRANTED AS TO THE ALLEGATIONS OF DUTIES OWED BY OR TO NON-PARTIES. THE ONLY PERTINENT ALLEGATIONS OF WRONGDOING TO ANY OF THE PLAINTIFFS ARE FOR THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FINANCIAL ELDER ABUSE. THE ONLY CAUSE OF ACTION THAT SUPPORTS AN AWARD OF ATTORNEY'S FEES IS THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FINANCIAL ELDER ABUSE. PLAINTIFFS HAVE ADEQUATELY ALLEGED MALICE AND OPPRESSIVE SUPPORTING THEIR ALLEGATIONS REGARDING PUNITIVE DAMAGES. THE ALLEGATIONS OF DUTIES OWED BY OR TO NON-PARTIES ARE NOT GERMANE TO ANY OF THE CAUSES OF ACTION. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

PLAINTIFFS HAVE ADEQUATELY ALLEGED COMPLIANCE OR, AT LEAST SUBSTANTIAL COMPLIANCE, WITH THE PREFILING REQUIREMENT AND FAILURE TO STRICTLY COMPLY WITH THE PREFILING REQUIREMENT IS NOT A BASIS TO SUSTAIN A DEMURRER. THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION ADEQUATELY ALLEGES WRONGDOING AS TO THE PLAINTIFFS BRINGING THAT CLAIM. DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF JEAN SETO'S MOTION TO COMPEL CHARLES THOMPSON TO APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION: ABSENT WRITTEN AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES, SEE CAL. CIV. PROC. CODE 2024.060, AND ABSENT ORDER OF THE COURT, ID. 2024.050, LAY "DISCOVERY PROCEEDINGS" SHALL BE "COMPLETE . . . ON OR BEFORE THE 30TH DAY . . . BEFORE THE DATE INITIALLY SET FOR THE TRIAL OF THE ACTION," ID. 2024.020(A). MOREOVER, UNLESS OTHERWISE ORDERED, "A CONTINUANCE OR POSTPONEMENT OF THE TRIAL DATE DOES NOT OPERATE TO REOPEN DISCOVERY PROCEEDINGS." ID. 2024.020(B). A TRIAL COURT HAS DISCRETION TO HEAR A MOTION TO COMPEL AFTER THE CUTOFF DATE, BUT ONLY UPON "TAKING INTO CONSIDERATION ANY MATTER RELEVANT TO THE LEAVE REQUESTED, INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, THE NECESSITY AND THE REASONS FOR THE DISCOVERY AND THE DILIGENCE OR LACK OF DILIGENCE OF THE PARTY SEEKING THE HEARING OF A DISCOVERY MOTION, AND THE REASONS THAT THE DISCOVERY MOTION WAS NOT HEARD EARLIER." PELTON-SHEPHERD INDUS., INC. V. DELTA PACKAGING PRODS, INC., 165 CAL.APP.4TH 1568, 1588 (2008) (CLEANED UP). SINCE PLAINTIFF DID NOT MOVE TO REOPEN DISCOVERY, DEFENDANT HAS NOT HAD THE OPPORTUNITY TO PROVIDE BRIEFING ON THESE ISSUES. ACCORDINGLY, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO ITS RENEWAL UPON FILING A MOTION TO REOPEN DISCOVERY. AS THE PREVAILING PARTIES, DEFENDANTS SHALL SUBMIT A FORM OF THE ORDER RECAPITULATING THIS ADOPTED RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CAL. R. CT. 3.1312 VIA EMAIL TO NGRAY@SHADESOFGRAY.LAW JUDGE PRO TEM: NAOMI GRAY, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

ORDER GRANTING PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS JEAN W. SETO, HM WONG GROUP, TINA WONG CHIN, ALEXANDER HANS WONG, AND ERIC KIM WONG'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. CALIFORNIA HAS A POLICY OF LIBERALLY PERMITTING AMENDMENTS TO PLEADINGS AT ANY STAGE OF THE PROCEEDING. (ATKINSON V. ELK CORP. (2003) 109 CAL.APP.4TH 739, 760.) SIMILARLY, CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 474 LIBERALLY ALLOWS DOE AMENDMENTS WHERE PLAINTIFFS WERE IGNORANT OF FACTS THAT WOULD SUPPORT A CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST THE NEW DEFENDANT, EVEN IF PLAINTIFFS KNEW THE IDENTITY OF THE DEFENDANT. (MCNAIR V. WORLDWIDE CHURCH OF GOD (1987) 197 CAL.APP.3D 363, 379.) THROUGH DEPOSITIONS OF DEFENDANTS KWOK HUNG SZETO, NIKKI SZETO, AND PATRICK SZETO, PLAINTIFFS LEARNED OF NEW FACTS THAT POTENTIALLY IMPLICATE CHARLES M. THOMPSON AND HIS LAW FIRM IN THE DISPUTE ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT. DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT SHOWN THAT THEY WILL BE PREJUDICED BY THE COURT ALLOWING THE FILING OF THE FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT BECAUSE TRIAL IS THREE MONTHS AWAY, THE NEW ALLEGATIONS ARISE FROM THE SAME CORE FACTS, AND ANY NEW DISCOVERY WILL BE LIMITED TO THE INVOLVEMENT OF THE LAWYERS IN THE ALLEGED MISCONDUCT. HOWEVER, ABSENT A RULING GRANTING PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO REOPEN DISCOVERY, THIS ORDER DOES NOT ENTITLE PLAINTIFFS TO SEEK ANY DISCOVERY RELATED TO THE NEW ALLEGATIONS OR THE NEW CAUSE OF ACTION IN THE FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT DECLINES TO RULE ON DEFENDANTS' EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS AS THE COURT ONLY RELIES UPON PARAGRAPHS 5, 8, 9, AND 10 OF THE DECLARATION OF JENNI E. KLOSE AND THE ATTACHED DEPOSITION TESTIMONY IN REACHING ITS DECISION. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: W. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Fourth Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-24-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jenni E. Klose, Esq., Law Offices of Jenni E. Klose, Telephone 707-921-5438, appearing for Plaintiff (CourtCall)
diane Pritchard, Esq., Vogl Meredith Burke, LLP., telephone 415-398-0200, appearing for Defendant(s) kwok Hung Szeto et al; (CourtCall)

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS JEAN SETO, JEAN SETO, JEAN SETO, JEAN SETO, TINA CHIN, ALEXANDER WONG, ERIC WONG's Motion For Leave To File Fourth Amended Complaint.

Plaintiffs Jean W. Seto, HM Wong Group, Tina Wong Chin, Alexander Hans Wong, and Eric Kim Wong's motion for leave to file fourth amended complaint is GRANTED. California has a policy of liberally permitting amendments to pleadings at any stage of the proceeding. (Atkinson v. Elk Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 739, 760.) Similarly, Code of Civil Procedure section 474 liberally allows Doe amendments where Plaintiffs were ignorant of facts that would support a cause of action against the new Defendant, even if Plaintiffs knew the identity of the Defendant. (McNair v. Worldwide Church of God (1987) 197 Cal.App.3d 363, 379.) Through depositions of Defendants Kwok Hung Szeto, Nikki Szeto, and Patrick Szeto, Plaintiffs learned of new facts that potentially implicate Charles M. Thompson and his law firm in the dispute alleged in the complaint. Defendants have not shown that they will be prejudiced by the Court allowing the filing of the fourth amended complaint because trial is three months away, the new allegations arise from the same core facts, and any new discovery will be limited to the involvement of the lawyers in the alleged misconduct. However, absent a ruling granting Plaintiffs' motion for leave to reopen discovery, this order does not entitle Plaintiffs to seek any discovery related to the new allegations or the new cause of action in the fourth amended complaint. The Court declines to rule on Defendants' evidentiary objections as the Court only relies upon paragraphs 5, 8, 9, and 10 of the Declaration of Jenni E. Klose and the attached deposition testimony in reaching its decision.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JEAN SETO's Motion To Compel Charles Thompson To Appear For Deposition

Absent written agreement of the parties, see Cal. Civ. Proc. Code 2024.060, and absent order of the court, id. 2024.050, lay "discovery proceedings" shall be "complete . . . on or before the 30th day . . . before the date initially set for the trial of the action," id. 2024.020(a). Moreover, unless otherwise ordered, "a continuance or postponement of the trial date does not operate to reopen discovery proceedings." Id. 2024.020(b). A trial court has discretion to hear a motion to compel after the cutoff date, but only upon "taking into consideration any matter relevant to the leave requested, including, but not limited to, the necessity and the reasons for the discovery and the diligence or lack of diligence of the party seeking the hearing of a discovery motion, and the reasons that the discovery motion was not heard earlier." Pelton-Shepherd Indus., Inc. v. Delta Packaging Prods, Inc., 165 Cal.App.4th 1568, 1588 (2008) (cleaned up). Since Plaintiff did not move to reopen discovery, Defendant has not had the opportunity to provide briefing on these issues. Accordingly, Plaintiff's motion is DENIED without prejudice to its renewal upon filing a motion to reopen discovery.

As the prevailing parties, Defendants shall submit a form of the order recapitulating this adopted ruling and in compliance with Cal. R. Ct. 3.1312 via email to ngray@shadesofgray.law

Judge Pro Tem: Naomi Gray, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion For Attorneys' Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Nov-01-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JEAN SETO, TINA CHIN, ALEXANDER WONG, ERIC WONG's Motion For Attorneys' Fees And Costs.

Cross-defendant Jean Seto's motion for attorneys' fees and costs is granted in part. Ms. Seto is awarded fees and costs of $21,716.50 against cross-complainants Kwok, Nikki and Patrick Szeto. While Ms. Seto is entitled to an award of fees per CCP 425.16(c)(1), case law establishes that the award is limited to reasonably incurred fees and costs. The court does not doubt that Ms. Seto's counsel spent the time that is listed on exhibit A to Ms. Klose's declaration. However, based on the court's familiarity with the underlying anti-SLAPP motion and this motion for fees, at the hourly rate of $350, 60 hours is the upper limit for the number of reasonably spent hours for filing and prosecuting both motions and thus Ms. Seto is entitled to a fee award of $21,000. None of the issues raised by either motion were particularly novel, intricate or difficult. The Szetos have not made any objections to Ms. Seto's request for $716.50 in costs.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR #11185, email:ADelgado@sftc.org, Reported. (D302)

Notice Of Special Motion And Special Motion To Strike
Off Calendar Jul-28-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Jack W. Weaver of the law offices of Welty Welty (707.433.4842) appears as counsel for plaintiff/cross-defendant/moving party

Attorney, Jenni E. Klose of the law offices of Jenni E. Klose (707.921.5438) appears as counsel for plaintiff/cross-defendant/moving party

Attorney, Joel Bierer of the law offices of Joel D. Bierer (415.383.5666) appears as counsel for defendant/Cross-complainant/opposing party

Attorney, Beth Tenney of the law offices of Williams & Gumbiner LLP (415.755.1880) appears as counsel for defendant/Cross-complainant/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF JEAN SETO's Special anti-SLAPP motion to strike both causes of action alleged in Kwok, Nikki, and Patrick Szeto's cross-complaint is GRANTED as to both causes of action. Ms. Seto satisfied her first prong burden that both causes of action arise from activity protected by the anti-SLAPP statute and the Szetos are unable to satisfy their second prong burden that either or both of their claims have minimal merit. Ms. Seto's statements regarding Richard Szeto's purported mismanagement of the investment entities-during her telephone call with Wei Yuan Chao and in her letter to Mrs. Chao and her husband Che Sheng Chao-constitute protected activity per CCP 425.16(e)(2) and are absolutely privileged per Civil Code 47(b)(2). Ms. Seto has shown that the statements concern the subject of the underlying dispute as alleged in the second amended complaint, i.e. the asserted mismanagement of the investment entities, and her threatened appropriate action in that context was in anticipation of litigation contemplated in good faith and under serious consideration. Prior to making the statements at issue, Ms. Seto met with the Szetos and informed them that she intended to investigate the legality of their conduct, intended to explore her legal rights as an investor and the rights of her co-investors, and was considering bringing legal action to ensure the entities were being properly managed. Moreover, Ms. Seto was certain at that point that legal action was the only means to ensure the entities were managed properly since the Szeto family had exclusive control over the entities. The Szetos have not submitted admissible evidence showing otherwise. Ms. Seto's evidentiary objections numbers 1 through 7 are sustained. Per Briggs v. Eden Council for Hope & Opportunity (1999) 19 Cal.4th 1106, 1123, Ms. Seto need not show that the statements complained of were in the public interest. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org. (D302)

Defendants Kwok, Nikki and Patrick Szeto's demurrer to all eight causes of action alleged in the second amended complaint is overruled as to the first through sixth and eighth causes of action and sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action. Plaintiffs have adequately alleged compliance or, at least substantial compliance, with the prefiling requirement and failure to strictly comply with the prefiling requirement is not a basis to sustain a demurrer. None of the claims alleged in the second amended complaint are uncertain. The seventh cause of action for fraud is not particularly pled as required by fraud pleading requirements. The eighth cause of action adequately alleges wrongdoing as to the plaintiffs bringing that claim. The prevailing party must submit a written [proposed] order tracking, verbatim, the Court's ruling and in accordance with C.R.C.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-02-2017 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES -ATTY. JACK W. WEAVER OF THE WELTY & WELTY LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-707-433-4842 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. BETH TENNEY OF THE WG LAW FIRM, TEL. 1-415-755-1880 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court modified its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants Kwok, Nikki, and Patrick Szeto's demurrer to the first amended complaint filed by plaintiffs Jean Seto, HM Wong Group, Tina Chin, Alexander Wong, and Eric Wong is off calendar. Plaintiffs are granted leave to file a second amended complaint no later than May 22, 1017. Plaintiffs' opposition memorandum states that plaintiffs desire to file a second amended complaint, which plaintiffs believe addresses many of the issues raised by the demurrer. The court's tentative are:, the allegations of demand futility and prior delivery of the first amended complaint that are contained in the first amended complaint adequately comply are sufficient to satisfy the demand and prefiling delivery requirements of Corporations Code 17709.02(a)(2) and thus those allegations withstand a demurrer. If, as it appears, Richard Szeto is alleged by plaintiffs to be the only manager of ARP-I, LLC and ARP-II, LLC and the wrongful conduct complained of is alleged to arise primarily from his conduct in the mismanagement of those entities, such allegations adequate for demand futility. In the absence of any California case law holding that the prefiling delivery requirement cannot be satisfied by an amended complaint and the ambiguity of the statutory language, this court holds that an allegation that a plaintiff delivered an amended complaint to a defendant prior to serving it is sufficient to satisfy the prefiling delivery requirement of 17709.02(a)(2). Doing so furthers substantial justice and is in accord with California policy that, where possible, cases should be resolved on their merits rather than on technical procedural failures. One of the leading authorities on California corporations law refers to the prefiling delivery requirement as "a purely mechanical one that is easily satisfied." (Marsh, Finkle and Sonsini, Marsh's California Corporations Law (4th ed. 2017) 15.11(E).). Moreover, case law construing other prefiling requirements such as those in FEHA and PAGA hold that those prefiling requirements can be satisfied by amended complaints. Defendants are not precluded from re-asserting their arguments regarding the demand and prefiling requirements. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189
